title: noccaea fendleri subsp. idahoense
intro: Noccaea fendleri subsp. idahoense, also known as Idaho pygmy wintergreen, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small, rounded leaves and white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that may help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Idaho pygmy wintergreen include its potential to reduce inflammation and pain, as well as its ability to support immune function and promote overall well-being. Noccaea fendleri subsp. idahoense is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
